feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-devportal-trial failed with: Forbidden on Azure
entry.content:
Creation Failed
Couldn't create an instance of service 'API Management, developer portal' with plan 'devportal-apiaccess'.
Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-devportal-trial failed with: Forbidden

On Azure Singapore BTP Integration Suite. Who do we contact to get this broker service fixed?

entry.title: Integration Suite issue with Deep Entity create: dependent entities missing
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing a rather interesting issue that I have never seen before. I have the following scenario: An integration flow in Integration Suite that connects to an OData service on-premise, with cloud connector setup (the Odata service is hosted on premise, ECC.

 

All is well but for one flow: I have an scenario that i will forward a payload like this one:

<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?><TransportEntitySet><TransportEntity><CodigoRota>15573914</CodigoRota><DataHoraImportacaoRota>2026-01-30T11:28:00</DataHoraImportacaoRota><DataHoraInicioRota>2026-01-30T16:22:00</DataHoraInicioRota><DataHoraFimRota>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimRota><NomeRota>0060012623</NomeRota><Status>2</Status><IdDeposito/><Entregas><Entrega><CodigoEntrega>118350098</CodigoEntrega><DataHoraChegada>2026-01-30T16:22:00</DataHoraChegada><DataHoraFimAtendimento>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimAtendimento><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352640</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352640</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794518</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Lorem Ipsum</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos></Entrega></Entregas><Entregas><Entrega><CodigoEntrega>118350099</CodigoEntrega><DataHoraChegada>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraChegada><DataHoraFimAtendimento>2026-01-30T16:23:00</DataHoraFimAtendimento><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352641</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352641</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794520</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Texto 01 / Texto 02 / Texto 03 / Texto 04</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos><Pedidos><Pedido><IdPedido>000352642</IdPedido><NumeroPedido>000352642</NumeroPedido><Remessa>0310794521</Remessa><ComentarioPedido>Texto 01 / Texto 02 / Texto 03 / Texto 04</ComentarioPedido><CodigosBarra/></Pedido></Pedidos></Entrega></Entregas></TransportEntity></TransportEntitySet>

As the OData adapter only accepts XML for POST methods, we'll send the payload as above as the result of the conversion of a JSON to XML, so looking at the XML seems correct. The definition of the service is as below (from the associations panel):

 

 

However when the OData adapter is called, the message is transformed to the following payload, where each of the composite (expanded) entities Entregas and Pedidos have only one entry, namely the last one.

I Thought it was a cardinality problem, but it doesn't seem like that. I'll try to assign referential constraints based on the id of the message but it should not have any.

Has anyone faced something similar? i think i'll reshape the service and maybe add some referential constraints

appreciate any inputs

Erick

entry.title: SAP Integraton Suite - Custom adaper (asynchronous) - send step
entry.content:
Dear SAP Integration Experts,

I am trying to develop custom adapter to be used in SAP Integration Suite for asynchronous processing and I would like to mainly use that adapter in a Send step in iFlow. I have tried almost everything and come up with:  Send is not supported for the adapter. I was able to get the adapter working with request-reply step and surely this can work but as this adapter is asynchronous in nature it would be great to be able to use it with Send step. All of the examples I have found use either simple iflow with end step and the custom adapter or with request reply step. What I would like to know is whether the send step can be used only with buill in SAP adapters (Kafka, JMS) and this is intentional and I have to live with request-reply step or it can be used and I am missing some configurations? Thanks a lot. Pavol

entry.title: Proper End-to-End Steps to Consume SAP Business Accelerator Hub APIs in SAP Integration Suite (OAuth
entry.content:
I’m trying to integrate SAP Integration Suite (CPI) with APIs from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

I initially used API keys via HTTP headers, but ran into 401/403 errors, which made it unclear:

  • when API keys are sufficient, and

  • when OAuth 2.0 is mandatory.

I’m also confused about the exact BTP services and steps required to obtain OAuth client credentials for real SAP S/4HANA Cloud APIs, especially compared to sandbox APIs that seem to work with API keys only.

I’m looking for a clear end-to-end explanation of:

  • When to use API Key vs OAuth 2.0

  • How sandbox APIs behave (read-only / mock data)

  • Exact BTP steps to obtain OAuth credentials for S/4HANA Cloud APIs

  • How to configure CPI correctly to consume these APIs

entry.title: SAP DRC cloud edition | non SAP billing system
entry.content:
Hi team,

I would like to know if it’s possible to have the below flow with SAP DRC cloud edition, for the submission of e-reporting data to the tax authorities? the goal is not to use the SAP S/4 Hana 

1.Non-SAP ERP

  • Generates UBL XML (for eInvoice, credit note, etc.)

SAP BTP (Integration Suite)

  • Receives the UBL file
  • Maps UBL → SAP DRC canonical format
  • Handles security, certificates, retries

3 SAP DRC Cloud Edition

  • Receives transformed document
  • Applies country-specific compliance rules
  • Manages submission, status, and audit trail

4Tax Authority

  • Accepts or rejects document
  • Response flows back the same way

entry.title: SAP DRC cloud edition | Non SAP billing system
entry.content:
Hi team,

I would like to know if it’s possible to have the below flow with SAP DRC cloud edition, for the submission of e-reporting data to the tax authorities? the goal is not use the SAP S/4 Hana 

1 Non-SAP ERP

  • Generates UBL XML (eInvoice, credit note, etc.)

3 SAP BTP (Integration Suite)

  • Receives the UBL file
  • Maps UBL → SAP DRC canonical format
  • Handles security, certificates, retries

3 SAP DRC Cloud Edition

  • Receives transformed document
  • Applies country-specific compliance rules
  • Manages submission, status, and audit trail

4 Tax Authority

  • Accepts or rejects document
  • Response flows back the same way

entry.title: Can not use the SAP Gateway Server (ES5) in the Learning 'Developing with SAP Integration Suite'
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I would like to ask for advice regarding Integration Suite.
"I am currently taking the SAP Learning Journey: 'Developing with SAP Integration Suite' using an SAP BTP Trial account.

While following the exercise 'Log on SAP Gateway Demo Server - ES5' within the unit 'Exploring the Capabilities of SAP Integration Suite', I noticed that the ES5 demo server has been decommissioned, as mentioned in this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/gateway-demo-signup.html

This has stalled my progress. To continue with the subsequent exercises as intended, what would be the best alternative.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Integration suites capability
entry.content: Error undefined: Activation of Trading Partner Management capability has failed. If the issue persists, learn to solve from integration experts

entry.title: Externalized parameters in BTP CPI
entry.content:
I am new to BTP CPI and working on externalized parameters.  I have created and deployed. an iFlow with external parameter "runtimeURL" and have provided the default value. 

Q1. Can we configure the value of the externalized parameter "runtimeURL" after deployment from Monitor → Manage Integration Content as I don't find any option to do so.

entry.title: Azure databricks Integration using Cloud Integration(SAP IS)
entry.content:
hi Experts,

We are trying to connect Azure databricks Integration using Cloud Integration(SAP IS).. but the database types supported by Cloud Integration capability are the following ... Could you please suggest regarding the alternate options available to establish connectivity from Cloud Integration to Azure databricks.... Thank you!

-- Ramu.

entry.title: Updates on C_CPI Rollout & Transition to 2026 Exam Format?
entry.content: Hello Community! I'm planning my certification path and was wondering if anyone has heard updates on when the next C_CPI rollout is expected? Any insights from those who have recently checked would be greatly appreciated

entry.title: Elimination of SAP PI PO systems
entry.content:
Hello. I'm creating this post so we can all share our opinions constructively.

I recently learned that SAP plans to eliminate SAP PI/PO systems to focus solely on Integration Suite.

As someone who works extensively with PO, I feel saddened and angry with SAP. After all these years of
working and continuously acquiring knowledge in PO, SAP seems to have decided to kick it to the curb.
All that knowledge feels like throwing it away. Similarly, SAP is forcing clients who have already invested
money in implementing PO to migrate to Integration Suite. I believe many clients don't want the cloud and prefer
to keep their servers on-premises for security or other reasons. However, SAP doesn't care and is forcing all
clients to move their infrastructure to the cloud... and of course, spend money again.
Personally, I find this despicable.

And although I work with PO, I also work with Integration Suite. I find IS awful, much more difficult,
since you have to develop the business logic by creating huge and complicated iFlows, while in PO,
integrations are generally point-to-point, making them easier to implement and understand.
And then there are options like the Monitor, which is rubbish compared to the PO monitor, where it shows
you all the messages, their log traces, payloads... without you having to do anything, by default.
In IS, if you want to see anything in the monitor, you have to develop it yourself to save headers,
log files, payloads, etc., wasting your time.

Anyway, I find it incredible that SAP has played this game with its customers, forcing them to switch.
And of course, it's forcing us, the employees, to learn another way of doing integrations, throwing away
our knowledge. (I just hope SAP doesn't do very well.)

What do you think?
What's your opinion?
Are you for or against it?

Let's see if we can start a thread to discuss this and get it to someone at SAP.

Thanks. Regards.

entry.title: Technical validation request: multiple copies of the same legacy system integrating simultaneously
entry.content:
Technical validation request: multiple copies of the same legacy system integrating simultaneously to S/4HANA (RISE) QAS, end-to-end SD flow (synchronous)

I’m designing an integration solution for a customer and would like your help validating technical feasibility — both the overall architecture and the specific integrations — before proceeding.

Scenario

  • Customer on SAP RISE with S/4HANA (QAS).
  • Up to 10 copies of the same legacy system (Instance 1 to Instance 10) integrating simultaneously against the same QAS environment.
  • Full Sales flow, synchronous: ATP Check → Sales Order → Delivery → PGI → Billing → Status Return.

Proposed architecture (summary)

  • Central hub on SAP BTP using SAP Integration Suite (iFlows per process), identifying each legacy copy via headers:
    • X-Sender-System (business logical system)
    • X-Sender-Instance (copy index: 1..10)
  • SAP API Management for authentication, rate limiting, and distinct credentials per instance.
  • On S/4HANA QAS:
    • Communication System and Communication Arrangement per instance (up to 10 in parallel).
    • Use standard S/4 OData APIs for the flow: API_ATP_CHECK, API_SALES_ORDER_SRV, API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV, API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV.
    • SAP Application Interface Framework (AIF) for functional monitoring and traceability, using CorrelationID + SenderInstance as key fields (to isolate reprocessing per instance).

Note: The proposal is fully synchronous end-to-end for testing; no messaging/events here.

Example flow (synchronous, high level)

  1. Legacy Instance n calls ATP; the hub validates headers/credentials, forwards to S/4, and returns the response.
  2. Sales Order creation via API_SALES_ORDER_SRV; confirmation returns synchronously to the same instance.
  3. Delivery creation and PGI via logistics APIs; confirmations return to the caller.
  4. Billing via API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV; document number is returned to the corresponding instance.
  5. The entire cycle is traceable in AIF and Integration Suite monitoring by instance (SenderInstance) and CorrelationID.

Questions for the community

  1. Feasibility on RISE (Cloud Private Edition):
    • Are there any platform, tenant, or contractual constraints in RISE that would prevent this hub pattern with multiple instances of the same legacy calling QAS in parallel?
  2. Coverage of S/4HANA OData APIs (RISE):
    • Do the listed OData APIs (ATP, Sales Order, Outbound Delivery, Billing) support the full, 100% synchronous flow in this context?
    • Are there process steps that typically require alternatives (e.g., BAPI/IDoc) even in synchronous scenarios?
  3. Scale of Communication Systems/Arrangements:
    • Is it feasible to have up to 10 Communication Systems/Arrangements active for the same SD scenario in QAS simultaneously, one per legacy instance, without practical limitations?
  4. Isolation in AIF:
    • Does AIF support isolating reprocessing per instance (via key fields SenderInstance + CorrelationID), ensuring reprocessing for one instance does not affect others running in parallel?
  5. iFlow parameterization:
    • Do iFlows with Externalized Parameters per instance (endpoints, credentials, toggles) work on the RISE BTP tenant as designed? Any best practices to separate 10 instances without “exploding” the number of artifacts?

Any additional guidance is welcome

  • Naming best practices (Communication System/Arrangement per instance).
  • Practical throughput/concurrency limits in QAS for parallel SD testing.
  • Lessons learned for 100% synchronous SD flows (especially for Delivery/PGI/Billing via OData).

entry.title: Access CDS View data through SAP Integration Suite for Power BI / Excel without embedding credential
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to expose data from an SAP CDS View through SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) so that it can be consumed from Power BI and Excel. It is compulsory to use Integration Suite.

The main constraint of the scenario is authentication.

The Power BI and Excel files will be shared with multiple users, so I cannot embed credentials (for example in VBA scripts or connection strings).
Instead, the idea is to expose the CDS data through Integration Suite as a controlled endpoint.

The desired architecture is:

 
Power BI / Excel
↓
SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)
↓
SAP Gateway OData Service (generated from CDS View)
 

The CDS View is already published as an OData service in SAP Gateway, and it works correctly when accessed directly.

What I have tried so far

I tried two different approaches in Integration Suite.

1. OData → OData Integration Flow

First I created an integration flow using OData adapters on both sides.

Configuration:

Sender adapter

OData V2
Operation: Query (GET)

Receiver adapter

 

OData V2
Resource path: <EntitySet>
Query options: ${header.CamelHttpQuery}
Authentication: Basic (credential stored in Integration Suite)

Goal: create a transparent OData proxy.

Result:

  • The iFlow deployed successfully.

  • I was able to retrieve the metadata from the CDS view through this approach.

  • PowerBI detected the Set BUT gave Internal Server Error (Error no500), so I could not access the info (same problem if tried in browser).

2. HTTPS Sender → HTTP Receiver proxy

I then attempted a (supposely) simpler HTTP proxy approach.

Sender:

HTTPS
Address:
/powerbi/cds02/*
 
Integration flow:
HTTPS Sender
↓
Start
↓
Content Modifier
↓
HTTP Receiver

Content Modifier headers:

CamelHttpPath = ${header.CamelHttpPath}
CamelHttpQuery = ${header.CamelHttpQuery}
 

The idea was to dynamically forward the request path and query string.
In SAP Cloud Integration, the CamelHttpQuery header contains the query parameters from the original request URL.

Receiver configuration:

Address: https://<backend>/sap/opu/odata/sap/<SERVICE_NAME>
Authentication: Basic

Result:

  • The iFlow deploys successfully.

  • However every request returns: An internal server error occured: The MPL ID for the failed message is : (number)

So in practice this HTTP proxy approach does not reach the CDS data at all.

Current status

Summary of what I achieved so far:

Approach Result
OData → ODataMetadata reached (column names got), but not data in it (internal server error)
HTTPS → HTTP proxyRequests fail with internal server error

Questions

  1. What is the recommended architecture to expose CDS data through Integration Suite for consumption by Power BI / Excel?

  2. Is using Integration Suite as an OData proxy the correct approach in this scenario?

  3. Are there special considerations for handling $metadata and query parameters when forwarding OData requests through CPI?

  4. Is there a better approach to solve the authentication constraint (no embedded credentials in Excel / Power BI files)?

Any guidance or examples of similar implementations would be greatly appreciated. I could not even find any tutorials or anything (involving Integration Suite), though I guess it is something used in many companies (it is my first time doing this).

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Transfer objects created in Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO. (Message-type and data-type)
entry.content:
Hi everyone !

    I'm working on a project migrating SAP PI/PO to Cloud Integration NEO (the migration from NEO to Cloud Foundry will be next year). To perform the migration using the migration tool, following the instructions provided by SAP, we created a BTP Trial account and imported all the objects from PI/PO. After the migration, I'm transferring the objects and iFlows to BTP NEO (Cloud Integration). However, Cloud Integration NEO doesn't have the option to import Data-Types or Message-Types that were created during the migration in the Integration Suite of the Trial account. My question is: has anyone done this transfer from Integration Suite to Cloud Integration NEO before? How do I transfer the data-types and message-types to NEO? And if I have to create them manually in NEO, how do I create these objects in their iFlows on Cloud Integration NEO ?

entry.title: Access Policies
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you please answer this question?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/access-policies/qaq-p/14030594

Thank you very much!

Best regards, Henrike

entry.title: Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - SAP S4 to Biztalk 2016
entry.content:
Hello Team,

While Connecting from SAP S4 Cloud to MS Biztalk 2016 it is failing with below error. Totally stucked up with this error for few weeks. SAP states to uplift SAP NCO connector to 3.1.6 it doesn't really help.

 

Table ARFCRSTATE line 0 out of range: table is empty - Stucked in SM58

Latest Update from SAP- Really issue is strange and fishy!!!

Logs:

 

MS REPLY:

 

 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rajesh PS

entry.title: Handling large file transfers (1GB+) in BTP-Integration Suite !
entry.content:
We are currently evaluating the migration of high-volume 'File-to-File' pass-through scenarios (exceeding 1GB) from SAP PI/PO to SAP BTP Integration Suite. While this is currently stable in our legacy environment, we need to ensure the Cloud Integration architecture can handle similar loads without memory bottlenecks.
when we try the same requirement in CPI ,it’s getting failed to read the file .

Any recommendations would be appreciated,thank you

-regards ,Bhaskar

Cloud Integration

entry.title: Could not fetch access token from the XSUAA
entry.content:
Dear experts,

could you please give me a hint how could I solve one interesting problem? We have implemented pretty simple iFlow on Integration Suite, which is started using HTTP Adapter and for which we have generated Credentials in BTP... But I try to start this iFlow from 3 different environments and I could start this flow just only from 2 environments even we are using the same environments...

In the traces for a call which we are not able to process I see these logs:

com.sap.it.security.multicloud.authentication.ResponseErrorHandlerImpl#anonymous#it-sap-servlet-container-16...Could not fetch access token from the XSUAA: HTTP 400 BAD_REQUEST Bad Request:...

com.sap.it.security.multicloud.authentication.UaaTokenBroker#anonymous#it-sap-servlet-container-16...com.sap.it.security.multicloud.authentication#na#na#na#na#The client certificate is not configured in a service key of a Process Integration Runtime service instance. Client certificate: ...

As I mention, I'm using the same credentials from all the environments and in 2 cases it is process and in 1 case I get this error...

BR,

Milan

entry.title: Connection to IXOS Server from CPI - Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My requirement is to store the PDF files into IXOS server which is an opentext server for archiving the files and once we send any file we will get a archive id and it should be updated in S4HANA . Could anyone provide inputs on how to establish connection to IXOS server i mean how to store files in IXOS server from CPI whether it is API based push or SFTP based push. 

 

Regards,

Pavan Kalyan Racha
